title: Margin guardrails for promotional SKUs
text: For hero SKUs with planned promotions, maintain contribution margin after discount of at least 18% unless inventory risk is high. If inventory cover exceeds 90 days, prioritize velocity over margin for 30 days, then revert.

title: Inventory posture by velocity band
text: Fast movers (top decile weekly units): target 21-35 days of cover. Medium movers: 35-56 days. Slow movers: reduce incoming POs immediately if cover exceeds 70 days; run targeted bundles before broad markdowns.

title: Category positioning: premium adjacency
text: When a SKU sits adjacent to premium brands, avoid deep list-price cuts; use limited-time offers and loyalty perks to protect price perception. Emphasize quality proof points in PDP copy.

title: Returns-sensitive categories
text: Apparel and footwear: size-fit content, comparison charts, and free exchanges materially reduce returns. If return rate exceeds category benchmark by 150bps, pause broad acquisition until PDP fixes ship.

title: Elasticity heuristics for mid-price bands
text: In the $40-$120 band, a 10% list price reduction typically yields 15-25% unit lift if demand is elastic and competitors are within 5% on comparable items. Validate with a 14-day holdout test when traffic allows.

title: Promotional ladder
text: Prefer ladder promotions (tiered spend thresholds) over flat site-wide discounts to protect AOV. If clearance risk is present, use channel-specific codes before homepage banners.

title: Measurement minimums
text: Any pricing or promo test requires pre-registered primary metric, minimum 7 days of stable traffic, and exclusion of major holidays unless the test explicitly targets holiday demand.

title: Competitive response playbook
text: If a primary competitor undercuts by more than 8% for more than 5 days, evaluate match-on-visibility SKUs only; otherwise improve bundle value and loyalty incentives to avoid race-to-bottom.
